purpose: Keep generated report and checkpoint state bounded, owned, classed, and replayable without deleting evidence.

retention_classes.description: Mutable local indexes needed for current evidence lookup; capped separately from hot report payloads.
Tiny replay pointer left at the original path after manifest-backed retention.
Immutable report snapshots that should be archived through the retention manifest when they dominate hot report bytes.
Current human-readable latest report views; should stay small and update existing flagship families instead of spawning variants.
Append-only local ledgers and candidate rows; large files must move through archive pointers or an explicit ledger compaction.
Generated report subdirectory payloads owned by the runtime-generated-state surface.
Digest-bound local model checkpoints; retained live only while referenced by current training/eval reports.
Checkpoint vocab/config/digest metadata paired with retained weights.
Generated logs, source replay fixtures, temporary report payloads, locks, flags, and binary sidecars under reports.
Checkpoint-adjacent delta, source, fixture, and sidecar payloads that are retained with the checkpoint family.

rules.large_report_snapshots: Archive through scripts/theseus_artifact_retention.py with exact replay pointers.
rules.checkpoints: Do not move checkpoints needed by current reports unless consumers can follow archive pointers.
rules.non_claim: Budget compliance is repo hygiene evidence, not model capability evidence.
